message.content:
<|channel>thought<channel|>### API Design: Resumable Benchmark Runner

To satisfy the requirement of a "resumable" runner within a "one heavy model server at a time" constraint, the architecture uses a **Sequential Task Queue** with a **State Store**.

#### 1. Core API Sketch

```csharp
// The result of a single completed benchmark run
public record BenchmarkResult(
    BenchmarkContext Context, 
    double Score, 
    DateTime CompletedAt,
    string LogPath);

// Interface for persistence (SQL, File, or NoSQL)
public interface IBenchmarkRepository
{
    Task<IEnumerable<BenchmarkContext>> GetPendingContextsAsync();
    Task<BenchmarkResult?> GetCompletedResultAsync(BenchmarkContext context);
    Task SaveResultAsync(BenchmarkResult result);
    Task MarkAsRunningAsync(BenchmarkContext context);
}

// The Orchestrator
public class BenchmarkRunner
{
    private readonly IBenchmarkRepository _repo;
    private readonly IModelServerClient _dockerClient; // Wraps Docker harness
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _singleServerLock = new(1, 1);

    public BenchmarkRunner(IBenchmarkRepository repo, IModelServerClient dockerClient)
    
    {
        _repo = repo;
        _dockerClient = dockerClient;
    }

    public async Task RunAllAsync(CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var pending = await _repo.GetPendingContextsAsync();

        foreach (var context in pending)
        {
            if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) break;

            // Ensure only one heavy model server runs at a time
            await _singleServerLock.WaitAsync(ct);
            try 
            {
                await ExecuteSingleBenchmark(context, ct);
